meta.title: Stem · botanical studio · Prague
meta.description: A small botanical studio in Prague. Hand-tied seasonal bouquets, romantic weddings, weekly subscriptions and monthly workshops. Same-day delivery in Prague.

delivery.strip: Same-day delivery in Prague · order before 14:00
delivery.title: Do we deliver to you?
delivery.lead: Same-day delivery in Prague (zones 1–10), next day everywhere else in Czechia.

hero.title: Seasonal flowers, & a quiet studio
hero.sub: Hand-tied seasonal bouquets, romantic weddings and a workshop once a month. We grow what we can — the rest from local Czech farms. Never anything flown in.

services.label: What we do
services.title: Four ways we work with flowers
services.lead: Pick what fits — order a bouquet today, set up a weekly delivery, learn to make your own, or plan a wedding with us.

s1.name: Bouquets
s1.desc: Hand-tied seasonal bouquets in four sizes. Same-day delivery across Prague.

s2.name: Subscriptions
s2.desc: Weekly, fortnightly or monthly fresh flowers — at home or in the office. Pause anytime.

s3.name: Workshops
s3.desc: Once a month at the studio. Small groups, real flowers, espresso and natural wine included.

s4.name: Weddings
s4.desc: Romantic, garden-grown weddings — bouquets, arches, full installations. 6–10 couples a season.

shop.meta_desc: Hand-tied seasonal bouquets delivered same day in Prague. Choose by occasion or size.
shop.label: Shop
shop.title: Hand-tied. Same day. In season.
shop.lead: Every bouquet is one of a kind, built around what arrived fresh that morning. Pick a size and an occasion — same-day delivery across Prague.
shop.lead_home: Hand-tied, wrapped in unbleached paper and delivered the same day across Prague. Each bouquet is one of a kind — built around what's freshest the day you order.
shop.p1_notes: A small bouquet for a quick gesture — sweet pea, ranunculus and garden mint. Perfect for someone special, or just because.
shop.p2_notes: A generous bouquet for the dining table or a thoughtful gift — peonies, lilac, dusty miller and eucalyptus.
shop.p3_notes: Our florist's pick of the week, built around what's most beautiful right now. The bouquet we'd take home ourselves.
shop.p4_notes: Our most extravagant bouquet — peonies, garden roses and sweet pea. Made to order; please allow 48 hours.
shop.p5_notes: Soft pastels — chamomile, white ranunculus and eucalyptus, finished with a cream silk ribbon.
shop.p6_notes: A late-summer dahlia mix in burgundy, peach and coral. Wild edges, no fillers.
shop.p7_notes: For sympathy — white peonies, lisianthus and olive branches. A handwritten card is included.
shop.p8_notes: A twilight palette of purple lilac, dusty pink ranunculus, smoke bush and scabiosa.
shop.p9_notes: Peak-season abundance — David Austin roses, sweet pea, chocolate cosmos and summer herbs.

sub_banner.title: Fresh flowers every week. Pause any time.
sub_banner.lead: Weekly, fortnightly or monthly delivery to your home or office. We pick what's freshest that morning. Plans from 540 Kč/week.

cart.title: Your bag
cart.empty:
Empty for now.
Pick a bouquet to start.

sub.label: Subscriptions
sub.title: Subscribe to fresh flowers
sub.lead: A subscription is the easiest way to keep fresh flowers in your home or office. Pause any time, change frequency or skip a delivery — no questions asked.

wed.label: Weddings & events
wed.title: Flowers that look just-picked
wed.p1: Romantic, garden-grown weddings — bridal bouquets, ceremony arches, table runs and full installations. Just 6–10 couples per season, so each gets our full attention.
wed.p2: It starts with a coffee. Then a moodboard, a flower-by-flower plan and a clear quote. Once your date is booked, we hold it — no overbooking, no shortcuts.

ws.label: Workshops
ws.title: Make something with your hands
ws.lead: Workshops once a month at our studio. Small groups (max 8), seasonal themes, espresso and a glass of natural wine all included.

w1.title: Spring hand-tied bouquet
w1.desc: Build a generous spring bouquet with peonies, ranunculus and our garden's first sweet pea. You'll go home with the bouquet you made and a technique you'll keep.

w2.title: Garden roses, foam-free
w2.desc: A summer arrangement at the peak of rose season. We work without floral foam, using kenzans and chicken wire instead — kinder to the planet and better for the design.

w3.title: Summer wreath
w3.desc: A wild summer wreath made of herbs, garden flowers and dried elements. It will age beautifully on your door for months.

story.title: A small botanical studio in Vinohrady
story.p1: Stem started as a stand at a Sunday market in 2019. Today we're five florists on Slezská. One rule: we only sell flowers we'd take home ourselves.
story.p2: We grow what we can on a quarter-hectare in Polabí — sweet pea, garden roses, dahlias and herbs. The rest comes from three local Czech growers.

t1.quote: The Sunday bouquet has become our Saturday ritual. The flowers last twice as long as anything from a supermarket — and they actually feel like the season we're in.
t1.name: Andrea

t2.quote: Eva designed our wedding flowers and I cried twice — once at the ceremony and once when I saw the bouquet that morning.
t2.name: Tereza & Michal

t3.quote: I went to the spring workshop, took my bouquet home and refilled the water for two weeks. I learned more in three hours than from a year on Pinterest.
t3.name: Lucia

t4.quote: The Garden bouquet I sent my mum for her birthday was unreasonably beautiful. She said it looked like a painting — and she's not wrong.
t4.name: Petra

contact.label: Visit the studio
contact.title: Slezská 30, Prague 2
contact.text: A 5-minute walk from Náměstí Míru — through the green door at the back of the courtyard. Open Tuesday to Saturday; outside those days drop by appointment.

cookies.text: Cookies help us give you a better experience.

wed_pg.meta_desc: Romantic, garden-grown wedding flowers — bouquets, ceremony arches, table runs and full venue installations.
wed_pg.title: Garden flowers. One day. Memories that last.
wed_pg.lead: Romantic, garden-grown weddings — bouquets, ceremony arches, table runs and full installations. Just 6–10 couples a season. Once booked, your date is held.
wed_pg.f1_p1: A small garden wedding for 60 guests at a renovated farmhouse north of Prague. Klára wanted only what was actually blooming on the day, so we built the whole celebration around what came in that week — peonies still tight, garden roses, the first sweet pea of the season and an enormous amount of lilac.
wed_pg.f1_p2: We built the ceremony arch foam-free on chicken wire and reused it on the head table after the ceremony. Bridal bouquet, three smaller posies for the bridesmaids, eight buttonholes — everything delivered before 9 a.m. and we stayed for set-up.
wed_pg.f2_p1: An autumn city wedding at Villa Pellé. Burgundy dahlias, terracotta amaranth, dried millet and copper beech — Anna sent us one Pinterest pin and said "make it look picked, not made". So we did.
wed_pg.f2_p2: Three full venue installations (entrance arch, ceremony backdrop, dinner ceiling), thirty taper candles and a cake table dressed with chocolate cosmos. The bouquet was just the right weight — and Anna held it correctly in exactly zero photos.
wed_pg.f3_p1: An elopement in a Karlštejn forest clearing. Eight guests, lots of moss and a single violinist. We brought one oversized bouquet, two boutonnieres and a small ground-laid arrangement around the wooden ceremony plinth.
wed_pg.f3_p2: Sweet pea, white peonies, fern and lily of the valley, all still cool from the morning. The bouquet was so loosely tied that petals fell with every step — exactly as we'd intended.
wed_pg.proc_title: From the proposal to the first dance
wed_pg.proc1_d: Free, 60 minutes. Tell us what you're imagining — bring photos, fragments, even a song.
wed_pg.proc2_d: We'll send a moodboard, a flower-by-flower plan and a clear quote. No surprises later.
wed_pg.proc3_d: A 25% deposit secures your day. We never overbook — once you're booked, you have our full attention.
wed_pg.proc4_d: We deliver, set up and stay for the first photos. You hold the bouquet — and most people get a little teary.
wed_pg.inq_lead: We reply to every brief within 48 hours. No bots, no templates.
wed_pg.f_msg_ph: Palette, vibe, the one image you can't get out of your head…

ws_pg.meta_desc: Workshops once a month at our studio. Small groups (max 8), seasonal themes, espresso and natural wine included.
ws_pg.title: A slow afternoon, sharp scissors and a glass of wine
ws_pg.lead: Workshops once a month. Small groups, seasonal themes, real flowers. You'll go home with the bouquet you made and a new technique.
ws_pg.strip: Max 8 people · 2.5 — 3 hours · all materials, espresso & wine included · 1 950 Kč
ws_pg.w1_desc: Build a generous spring bouquet with peonies, ranunculus and our garden's first sweet pea. You'll learn the spiral grip, the natural "just-gathered" shape and how to keep the bouquet alive for two weeks at home.
ws_pg.w2_desc: A summer arrangement at the peak of rose season. We work foam-free with kenzans and chicken wire — kinder to the planet and better for the design. You'll go home with the technique and a vase to keep.
ws_pg.w3_desc: A wild summer wreath of herbs, garden flowers and dried elements. It will age beautifully on your door for months — and almost certainly become the most photographed thing about your home.
ws_pg.w4_desc: A long, low arrangement built for a dinner table — dahlias, amaranth and copper beech. We design for the conversation, not the photo.
ws_pg.w5_desc: Dried hydrangea, wheat, eucalyptus pods and gomphrena — a wreath that lasts the whole season. You'll learn the layering technique that stops everything looking like a craft-store kit.
ws_pg.w6_desc: A traditional Advent wreath, redesigned. Foraged greenery, beeswax candles, dried fruit and smoke bush. Includes a follow-up "refresh" pack you can pick up in week three.
ws_pg.incl_lead: We don't believe in upsells. The price you see is the price you pay — including the bouquet you take home, made from our genuine retail-quality flowers, not leftovers.
ws_pg.incl_1: All flowers, foliage and supplies — straight from our farm and Czech growers, never wholesale leftovers.
ws_pg.incl_2: Tools to borrow — secateurs, kenzans, twine and ribbons. Ours are sharp.
ws_pg.incl_3: Espresso, herbal tea and a glass of natural wine when we sit down at the end.
ws_pg.incl_4: A printed care guide so the bouquet you made survives more than three days.
ws_pg.incl_5: 15% off your next bouquet, valid for one month.
